story.from: epic.owner-harness's fifth declared piece (epic.owner-harness.json), and bdo's standing amendment of the 'bdo merges' hard rule (chat stamp, 2026-06-11, his words: he named PR-merging performative, twice). The organ was then built and admitted real — merge-node.claude.v1, node_real adm.852e04202b05 (bdo, 2026-06-12, 'fix the root cause and ship') — and has since landed ~78 PRs to main. Every one of those landings happened off this atom: the piece was declared, the organ was built and stamped, but atom.merge-node.v0 was never authored or run through the pipeline.
story.framing: The merge-node is the hand that lets bdo step out of the merge seat: an independent agent that lands a confirmed-arc PR it did not author, once the suite is green, executing bdo's confirm-arc rather than authorizing the merge itself (D-4, the same shape as the disposer executing the auto-admit fence). Its eyes are loop/merge.py (land-readiness per arc, a read-only fold over the digest); its hand is the PR pen's land verb (.claude/skills/branch-ritual/pr.py land). The divergence this atom closes is sharp and self-referential: the organ whose whole law is 'no one signs their own line' was itself the one writer with no atom drawn for it and — at first — no admission either (the 22 unadmitted landings mock-shame caught were exactly this self-asserted identity). The lander never landed itself. This atom is the retrospective backing that draws its line: it brings an already-real, already-running organ onto the log under the arc that declared it, so owner-harness reads true instead of reading 'unbuilt' over a node that has merged most of main.
story.work: Nothing is built here — the organ exists and is real. The work is the recording itself, honestly flagged: (1) this atom file as the merge-node's birth-on-the-log, citing the live organ (loop/merge.py + the PR pen land verb), its node_real admission (adm.852e04202b05), and the amended hard rule; (2) the §10 divergence it resolves — a confirmed arc (adm.728a87a9ca48) harbouring a piece that read 'not-yet-present' over a node that landed ~78 PRs — named, not papered over; (3) the stale epic glue surfaced for bdo: epic.owner-harness.json still says 'Until bdo admits the node real (--by bdo)... it merges nothing and he still merges', which his 2026-06-11/06-12 stamps already overtook — a needs-you note, never a silent edit of his steering surface.
story.need: No new owner stamp for the organ: bdo already admitted it real (adm.852e04202b05) and already amended the hard rule. The owner-stamp on this atom is satisfied by the confirmed arc (adm.728a87a9ca48). What remains is an independent value-gate judge that did not author this atom (no one signs their own line), and bdo's eventual call on whether the stale epic glue should be re-steered.
story.value: epic.owner-harness completes truthfully: the four pieces that read landed are joined by the fifth, and the piece that read 'unbuilt' stops contradicting a node that has merged most of main. The organ that enforces 'no one signs their own line' stops being the one writer that never had its own line drawn — the lander is on the log like everything it lands.
story.text: From owner-harness's fifth piece and bdo's stamp retiring his own merge seat, I need the live, already-real merge-node recorded on the log as the atom the arc declared — because an organ that lands most of main while its own piece reads 'unbuilt' is the exact off-log divergence the system exists to refuse, and the lander must hold its own line like every line it lands.

risk.rationale: The atom writes one record onto the log (append-only, superseding never erasing) and touches nothing the organ does not already touch — the merge-node itself is built, tested, and admitted real outside this atom. built_at_risk is honestly true: the work this atom records was built and stamped before the atom was authored (the off-log fact it exists to correct). It declares no new capability and asks for no new stamp; it draws a line that was owed.

briefing.headline: the lander gets landed
briefing.value: Your owner-harness arc reads 4 of 5 landed with the fifth piece 'unbuilt' — over the merge-node, the very organ you stamped to merge in your place, which has since landed ~78 PRs. This draws its line on the log so the arc completes truthfully and the node that enforces 'no one signs their own line' finally has its own.
briefing.why_now: You confirmed owner-harness (adm.728a87a9ca48) and admitted the merge-node real (adm.852e04202b05); the only thing missing was the piece on the log. Carrying the arc all the way through means closing this last gap rather than leaving the lander self-asserted.
briefing.if_accepted: owner-harness's pieces all resolve (four landed, the mirror superseded-forward into surface-registry, this fifth recorded); the merge-node stops being the off-log organ mock-shame flags.
briefing.if_rejected: The merge-node keeps landing main while its own piece reads 'unbuilt' — the arc you confirmed never reads true, and the one organ whose law is self-restraint stays the one with no line of its own.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Routine and reversible: one record, superseded by admission if wrong, the log never depending on it. The organ is unaffected either way — this only decides whether its line is drawn.
briefing.mechanism: loop/merge.py (the eyes: land-readiness per arc, a read-only fold over loop/digest.py) + .claude/skills/branch-ritual/pr.py land (the hand: the hard-gated epic→main land an independent merge-node runs after bdo confirms the arc); node_real adm.852e04202b05 (merge-node.claude.v1); the amended 'merge-node lands; bdo confirms arcs' hard rule (CLAUDE.md, 2026-06-11). This atom appends one birth record; it writes no new merge path.
reading: CLAUDE.md (the 'merge-node lands; bdo confirms arcs' hard rule)

source_artifacts: epic.owner-harness.json (the fifth declared piece, glue stale on realness)

_note: This file is the atom's birth record and content-hash source. It is never mutated (D-5). It is a retrospective backing: the merge-node organ it records was built and admitted real (adm.852e04202b05) before this atom existed — built_at_risk is honestly true — and this draws the line owner-harness's fifth piece declared but never received. Story fields follow the v2 form bdo reviewed on issue #7.
